question: Is the showroom background and red sports car from Input 1 preserved with the original composition?
Is the anime character from Input 2 clearly recognizable in the generated image (e.g., outfit, hair, helmet)?
Is the character placed as a standee to the front-right of the sports car?
Does the character on the standee retain the original anime art style (2D illustration) without appearing photorealistic?
Does the standee object exhibit visible side thickness (cardboard layers) to indicate it is not a flat digital overlay?
Is there a visible base support structure (e.g., brace, foot plate) at the bottom of the standee?
Are subtle halftone printing dots or a printed texture visible on the standee's surface?
Does the standee surface show slight specular reflections consistent with glossy cardboard?
Is the wheel rim from Input 3 recognizable (spoke design preserved) in the scene?
Has the wheel rim been transformed into a complete spare tire (rim surrounded by rubber sidewall/tread)?
Is the spare tire placed in the background, leaning against the white wall?
Does the spare tire align correctly with the perspective of the wall and floor (not appearing to float or tilt unnaturally)?
Do the standee and spare tire cast realistic contact shadows on the floor?
Are reflections of the added elements (standee and tire) visible on the polished showroom floor?
Are faint reflections of the standee or spare tire visible on the red sports car's glossy paint?
Do the highlights on the spare tire and standee match the direction and intensity of the showroom's ceiling lighting?
Are the brightness and color temperature of the added elements unified with the showroom's environment?
Is a subtle film grain texture applied across the entire image?
Do the standee and spare tire appear at a plausible life-size scale relative to the sports car?
Is the image free from severe artifacts, such as jagged edges around the standee or anatomical distortions?
